text: Last class: each party shall deliver the goods, and confidential information must not be disclosed.
Today: Q3 was significantly higher than Q2, and the target was exceeded by a substantial margin.
Q3 was significantly higher than Q2.
March was the highest figure of the year.
The premium segment grew twice as fast as the standard one.
The target was exceeded by a substantial margin.
The figures were reported by finance.
The target was exceeded in the third quarter.
The data is presented in the chart below.
I have been through the dashboard. The Q3 figure was the highest of the year, significantly higher than Q2, and well above the benchmark.
Good. And the breakdown by segment? I read that the premium one grew twice as fast as the standard, and that the target was exceeded.
Exactly. March was an outlier, far above every other month, and the cost-to-income ratio was lower than last year.
And Q4? What was the variance against the baseline?
Q4 was marginally lower than Q3, but higher costs were offset by stronger sales, so it stayed above the threshold.
Then I am ready. Q3 was the highest figure, the target was exceeded, and all figures were reported by finance. I will present it to the board tomorrow.
The premium segment is twice as large as the standard one.
All figures were reported by finance and are presented in the chart.

Maria Claudia, it is David. I have just been through the quarterly dashboard, so let me walk you through the numbers before the board meeting. First, the headline figure. Q3 was the highest figure of the year, significantly higher than Q2, and well above the industry benchmark. The target was exceeded by a substantial margin, so this is a strong quarter. Second, the breakdown by segment. The premium segment grew twice as fast as the standard one and was the most profitable metric on the board. March stood out as an outlier, far above every other month, and the cost-to-income ratio was lower than last year. Third, Q4. Q4 dipped slightly; it was marginally lower than Q3, but still substantial. Higher costs were offset by stronger sales, so it stayed comfortably above the threshold, and the variance against the Q1 baseline was minimal. One last thing, and you know this is the habit to watch: keep the passive when you report the numbers. Say the target was exceeded, the figures were reported by finance, the data is presented in the chart. Look at it tonight and call me in the morning.

Right, let me make sure I can present these results before the board meeting. David walked me through the dashboard, so let me say it back in my own words. First, the headline. Q3 was the highest figure of the year, significantly higher than Q2 and well above the benchmark, and the target was exceeded by a substantial margin. Then the breakdown by segment: the premium segment grew twice as fast as the standard one, it was the most profitable metric, and March was an outlier, far above every other month. As for Q4, it was marginally lower than Q3 but still substantial; higher costs were offset by stronger sales, so it stayed above the threshold, and the variance against the baseline was minimal. And the habit I always have to fix is the passive. I want to say the target was exceeded, not the target exceeded; the figures were reported by finance; the data is presented in the chart. So tomorrow, when I present to the board, I will be precise: a comparative for each pair, a superlative for the top figure, a degree modifier for the size, and the passive for every number. Then I will take their questions.

First, the raw figures arrive from finance, and you check them against the baseline. Then you compare the quarters: which figure is the highest, and how much higher Q3 is than Q2. Next, you look at the breakdown by segment and spot any outlier or discrepancy. After that, you check whether higher costs were offset and whether the result passed the threshold. Finally, the dashboard is prepared, and the numbers are presented to the board, with every figure reported in the passive.
Q3 was significantly higher than Q2.
March was the highest figure of the year.
The premium segment is twice as large as the standard one.
The target was exceeded by a substantial margin.
The figures were reported by finance.
March was an outlier, far above every other month.
All figures were reported by finance and are presented in the chart.
